body.title: agent_datacenter: agentctl init — portable rack bootstrap on clean machine
body.result: agent_datacenter/cli/agentctl.py: agentctl init (dirs+config+postgres-detect+imap-detect+skeleton start) + agentctl status. Wired via rack.py entry point. click>=8.0 added to pyproject.toml.
body.description:
The 'init at work' story: pip install agent_datacenter && agentctl init bootstraps a working empty rack on a clean machine (ubuntu + macOS).

Problem: agent_datacenter must work at work — a fresh machine, no igor, no TheIgors. agentctl init must produce a running rack from scratch.

Proposed shape:
- agent_datacenter/cli/agentctl.py (click-based CLI, registered in pyproject.toml scripts)
- agentctl init command sequence:
  1. Create ~/.agent_datacenter/ runtime dir
  2. Create datacenter_logs/ hierarchy: datacenter_logs/{postgres,skeleton,imap}/ 
  3. Write device_config.toml with defaults
  4. Check Postgres: if pg_ctl found → use local; if Docker available → use Docker Postgres; else warn + continue
  5. Start IMAP server (Dovecot if installed; Python stub as fallback)
  6. Start skeleton (MCP aggregator on localhost:7788 or configured port)
  7. Print: 'Rack is up. Devices: [skeleton]. Channels: [Shared]. Type agentctl status to verify.'
- agentctl status: calls rack.health() and prints result
- Idempotent: re-running init on existing install is safe

Affected files:
- ~/dev/src/agent_datacenter/agent_datacenter/cli/__init__.py (new)
- ~/dev/src/agent_datacenter/agent_datacenter/cli/agentctl.py (new)
- ~/dev/src/agent_datacenter/pyproject.toml ([project.scripts] entry)

Design rules:
- oop-first: init logic in agent_datacenter.rack or agent_datacenter.skeleton; cli is thin wrapper
- no-sqlite: init creates no SQLite databases
- docs-in-code: agentctl --help output is the documentation

Scope boundary:
IN: agentctl init, agentctl status, datacenter_logs/ hierarchy creation, Docker Postgres fallback
OUT: device registration (devices self-register on startup); SWADL/Igor-specific setup (separate)

Test plan:
- docker run --rm ubuntu:22.04 pip install -e /path/to/agent_datacenter && agentctl init exits 0
- After init: agentctl status shows skeleton online
- Re-running agentctl init on existing install exits 0, no duplicate processes
